name: Comoros

visa_free.notes: Visa-free entry for Kenyan passport holders announced with immediate effect by President Azali Assoumani in joint communiqué with Kenyan President Ruto on 6 July 2023. No specific maximum stay duration stated in the communiqué. Reciprocal: Kenya also committed to removing visa requirement for Comorian travel-document holders before end of 2023.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Comoros law (Loi n°88-025, Art. 2) requires all foreign nationals to hold a valid passport and a Comorian visa. Visas are available on arrival at the airport or port. Tourist visa: up to 45 days; short-stay visa: up to 3 months; transit visa: up to 5 days. The UN Mission page (official) lists 45-day visa at 60 EUR; the Wikipedia Visa policy of the Comoros article (citing IATA Timatic) states 30 EUR for a 45-day visa on arrival. The discrepancy between 30 EUR and 60 EUR could not be resolved from an authoritative Comoros government source — 60 EUR is from the official Comoros UN Mission page and is retained here pending clarification.

visa_policy.notes: Comoros law (Loi n°88-025, justice.gouv.km) requires all foreign nationals to hold a valid passport and a Comorian visa. Visas available on arrival for tourist stays (max 45 days), short stay (up to 3 months), and transit (up to 5 days). Long-stay visa (over 3 months) must be applied for in advance via Council of Ministers decision. Kenya is the only nationality confirmed visa-free via an official Comoros source (Presidency communiqué July 2023). No official e-visa portal or ETA system exists as of June 2026. Visa fee: 60 EUR per UN Mission (official); 30 EUR per IATA Timatic/Wikipedia — discrepancy unresolved.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of valid diplomatic, official, or service passports of India may enter, stay, and transit Comoros without a visa for up to 90 days. Agreement between the Government of the Republic of India and the Government of the Union of Comoros on the exemption of visa for holders of diplomatic, official/service passports. Signed 11 October 2019; entered into force 1 March 2021.

Holders of valid diplomatic passports, service passports, and passports for public affairs of China may enter Comoros without a visa for a stay of less than 30 days. Mutual visa exemption agreement between the People's Republic of China and the Union of Comoros; entered into force 26 February 2016.

Nationals of Morocco holding diplomatic or service passports valid for at least six months are authorized to enter, stay, exit, and transit through Comoros without a visa for a period not exceeding 90 days within a period of 180 days from the date of first entry. Members of diplomatic missions and consular representations accredited to Comoros and their household family members holding diplomatic or service passports may enter, stay, and transit without a visa for the entire period of accreditation. Agreement signed in Addis Ababa on 11 February 2026 by Minister Nasser Bourita and Comorian FM Mbae Mohamed; reported to enter into force March 2026.

cbi.notes: No active official Citizenship-by-Investment program found. Comoros previously operated an Economic Citizenship Program authorized by 2008 legislation. Non-official sources indicate it was terminated circa 2013–2017. US State Department page for Comoros (travel.state.gov) warns that 'economic citizen' passports (ECPs) do not confer right of return to Comoros and holders may be stateless — confirming the program is no longer officially operative. No official Comoros government website currently advertises an active CBI program.

agreements: AU Free Movement Protocol (signed by Comoros; not ratified; protocol not yet in force as of 2026)

COMESA (Common Market for Eastern and Southern Africa) — member state; COMESA 1984 Protocol on Gradual Relaxation and Elimination of Visa Requirements; COMESA Free Movement Protocol (1998) signed by only 4 member states as of 2025 — Comoros implementation status not confirmed on official sources

validation_notes: VALIDATED 2026-06-13. Re-fetched all core official sources.
Kenya visa-free (beit-salam.km): CONFIRMED — communiqué explicitly states immediate removal of visa obligation for Kenyan travel document holders; no max stay duration specified in communiqué.
Visa on arrival for all nationalities (un.int): CONFIRMED — fee 60 EUR for 45-day tourist visa per UN Mission page.

Loi n°88-025 (justice.gouv.km): CONFIRMED — Art. 2 requires visa for all foreigners; visa types (transit 5d, tourist 45d, short-stay 3m, long-stay >3m) confirmed; fee amounts not in the law itself (set by Finance Law).

India-Comoros diplomatic passport agreement (mea.gov.in/bvwa-passport): CONFIRMED — row 105, signed 11 Oct 2019, in force 1 Mar 2021, 90 days, Diplomatic/Official/Service. Direct PDF URL remains 404 but list page is authoritative.
China-Comoros diplomatic passport agreement (cs.mfa.gov.cn): CONFIRMED — entry #30, diplomatic/service/public affairs passports, in force 26 Feb 2016, max 30 days.

Morocco-Comoros agreement (diplomatie.ma): CONFIRMED signed 11 Feb 2026; reported to enter into force March 2026. diplomatie.ma SSL certificate error prevented direct WebFetch; confirmed via news search (moroccoworldnews.com, yabiladi.com, apanews.net reporting same date and terms). Entry into force date March 2026 added as note — now likely in force.
No official Comoros e-visa portal found: CONFIRMED — no evisa.km or government immigration portal operational as of June 2026.
CBI status note strengthened: US State Department (travel.state.gov) explicitly warns economic citizen passports (ECPs) do not confer right of return, corroborating program termination.
CHANGED: Added Morocco agreement entry-into-force month (March 2026) to conditions note, sourced from news reports consistent with MFA announcement.
CHANGED: Added US State Department ECP warning to CBI notes for stronger corroboration of termination.
NO CHANGE to classification structure — all entries verified as correctly placed (ordinary-passport grants in visa_policy; passport-type restrictions in conditional_access).
gaps: No dedicated official Comoros immigration or Ministry of Foreign Affairs English-language website was found or accessible.
Kenya visa-free: no specific maximum stay duration stated in the July 2023 communiqué.
Full enumerated list of bilaterally visa-exempt nationalities for ordinary passports (beyond Kenya) could not be verified from official Comoros government sources.
Visa fee discrepancy between IATA Timatic/Wikipedia (30 EUR) and UN Mission Comoros (60 EUR for 45 days) remains unresolved — 60 EUR is retained as it comes from the official UN Mission page.
COMESA Free Movement Protocol implementation status for Comoros not confirmed from official Comoros sources.
AU Free Movement Protocol: exact date of Comoros signature not confirmed.
No official residence-by-investment or golden visa program found.
No official digital nomad, skilled worker, or fast-track visa scheme found.

Morocco-Comoros agreement (Feb 2026): entered into force March 2026 per news reporting; no official Comoros government source confirmed ratification or exact EIF date — diplomatie.ma SSL error prevented direct page verification.

Libya diplomatic/official/service passport waiver (max 45 days) mentioned on Wikipedia visa policy page but no official bilateral source (Libya MFA or Comoros MFA) accessible to confirm — not recorded.
OCI cardholder entry to Comoros: no official source found — not recorded.
US/Schengen/UK visa holder conditional entry: referenced by third-party aggregators but no official Comoros government source found to confirm — not recorded.
India-Comoros agreement original PDF (mea.gov.in/Images/CPV/visa-exemption/Exemption_UOC_visa.pdf) returns 404; only list-page reference available.

outbound_official.note: No official Comoros government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. The Comoros Ministry of Foreign Affairs website (diplomatie.gouv.km) fails DNS resolution and is not reachable; the alternative MFA portal (diplomatie-comores.org) is also unreachable (ERR_TUNNEL_CONNECTION_FAILED via Firecrawl). The gouvernement.km domain likewise returns ECONNREFUSED. The UN Mission site (un.int/comoros) covers only inbound visa requirements. No search results reveal any official Comoros government page that enumerates countries where Comorian ordinary-passport holders may travel without a prior consular visa.

sources_checked.description: Official Comoros Ministry of Justice — Loi n°88-025 on conditions of entry and stay of foreigners in Comoros
Official Comoros Presidency (Beit-Salam) — Joint communiqué with Kenya July 2023, announcing mutual visa-free travel
Permanent Mission of the Union of Comoros to the United Nations — official visa/passport page; lists visa-on-arrival fees: 45 days = 60 EUR, 1 year = 250 EUR, 10 years = 500 EUR

India Ministry of External Affairs — Countries with operational Visa Exemption Agreement for holders of Diplomatic/Official/Service passports; Comoros listed as row 105, agreement signed 11 Oct 2019, in force 1 Mar 2021, 90 days
China Ministry of Foreign Affairs — List of Agreements on Mutual Visa Exemption; Comoros listed as entry #30, in force 26 Feb 2016, diplomatic/service/public affairs passports, max 30 days

Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs (diplomatie.ma) — Morocco-Comoros visa exemption agreement for diplomatic and service passport holders signed 11 February 2026 in Addis Ababa; entered into force March 2026; 90 days in 180. SSL error prevented direct fetch; terms confirmed via consistent reporting from moroccoworldnews.com, yabiladi.com, and apanews.net.
US State Department — Comoros visa reciprocity page; warns economic citizen passports (ECPs) do not confer right of return to Comoros, corroborating CBI program termination

Wikipedia — Visa policy of the Comoros; non-official aggregator; notes 30 EUR on-arrival fee (IATA Timatic) and Libya diplomatic passport waiver (unconfirmed from official source). Used for cross-reference only.
